mnemonics: SCATTER: See Correlations And Trends To Evaluate Relationships
keyPoints: A scatter plot displays the relationship between two continuous variables.
The pattern of points can reveal the direction (positive/negative), form (linear/non-linear), and strength of a relationship.
Outliers are easily identified as points that fall far from the general cloud of data.
commonMistakes: Assuming correlation implies causation from a scatter plot alone.
Overplotting: drawing conclusions when many points are stacked on top of each other, obscuring the true density and relationship.
